title: Percentile and quantile distribution
template: SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY value) as p25, PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY value) as median, PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY value) as p75, PERCENTILE_CONT(0.90) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY value) as p90, PERCENTILE_CONT(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY value) as p95, PERCENTILE_CONT(0.99) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY value) as p99, MAX(value) as max_value FROM metrics
anti_patterns: reporting only the mean for skewed distributions (revenue, latency, LTV) — mean is pulled by outliers; median and p95/p99 are more actionable
PERCENTILE_DISC returns an actual row value; PERCENTILE_CONT interpolates — use CONT for continuous metrics

title: Z-score outlier detection
template: WITH stats AS (SELECT AVG(metric_value) as mean_val, STDDEV(metric_value) as stddev_val FROM metrics) SELECT m.*, (m.metric_value - s.mean_val) / NULLIF(s.stddev_val, 0) as z_score, CASE WHEN ABS((m.metric_value - s.mean_val) / NULLIF(s.stddev_val, 0)) > 3 THEN 'outlier' WHEN ABS((m.metric_value - s.mean_val) / NULLIF(s.stddev_val, 0)) > 2 THEN 'suspect' ELSE 'normal' END as outlier_flag FROM metrics m CROSS JOIN stats s
anti_patterns: applying z-score to non-normal distributions — z-score assumes normality; use IQR method (Q3 + 1.5*IQR) for skewed data like revenue or LTV
|z| > 3 is the standard threshold for outliers; adjust to > 2 for stricter detection

title: IQR-based outlier detection (robust, non-parametric)
template: WITH bounds AS (SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY value) as q1, PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY value) as q3 FROM metrics), iqr AS (SELECT q1, q3, q3 - q1 as iqr, q1 - 1.5*(q3-q1) as lower_fence, q3 + 1.5*(q3-q1) as upper_fence FROM bounds) SELECT m.*, i.lower_fence, i.upper_fence, CASE WHEN m.value < i.lower_fence OR m.value > i.upper_fence THEN 'outlier' ELSE 'normal' END as outlier_flag FROM metrics m CROSS JOIN iqr i
anti_patterns: using 1.5*IQR for extreme outlier detection — use 3.0*IQR for extreme outliers only

title: Pearson correlation between two metrics
template: SELECT CORR(metric_a, metric_b) as pearson_r, REGR_SLOPE(metric_b, metric_a) as slope, REGR_INTERCEPT(metric_b, metric_a) as intercept, REGR_R2(metric_b, metric_a) as r_squared, COUNT(*) as n FROM metrics_table
anti_patterns: correlation = causation — always state this is associative
running CORR on time series without detrending — two upward trends will show near-perfect correlation even if unrelated (spurious correlation)

title: Simple linear regression (slope and intercept in SQL)
template: SELECT REGR_SLOPE(y_value, x_value) as slope, REGR_INTERCEPT(y_value, x_value) as intercept, REGR_R2(y_value, x_value) as r_squared, COUNT(*) as n, AVG(x_value) as avg_x, AVG(y_value) as avg_y FROM regression_data
anti_patterns: interpreting R² alone without checking residuals — a high R² can still mask a poor fit on non-linear data

title: Moving average (simple and exponential proxy)
template: SELECT date, value, AVG(value) OVER (ORDER BY date ROWS BETWEEN 6 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) as sma_7d, AVG(value) OVER (ORDER BY date ROWS BETWEEN 29 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) as sma_30d, AVG(value) OVER (ORDER BY date ROWS BETWEEN 89 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) as sma_90d FROM daily_metrics ORDER BY date
anti_patterns: using RANGE BETWEEN instead of ROWS BETWEEN on dates — RANGE groups equal values, which can include wrong rows when dates repeat
reporting moving average without flagging that early rows have fewer data points (warm-up period)

title: Standard deviation and variance for metric volatility
template: SELECT segment, AVG(daily_value) as mean, STDDEV(daily_value) as stddev, VARIANCE(daily_value) as variance, STDDEV(daily_value) / NULLIF(AVG(daily_value), 0) as coefficient_of_variation, MIN(daily_value) as min_val, MAX(daily_value) as max_val FROM daily_metrics GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY coefficient_of_variation DESC
anti_patterns: using STDDEV (sample) vs STDDEV_POP (population) interchangeably — use STDDEV for samples, STDDEV_POP only when you have the full population

title: Histogram bucketing with WIDTH_BUCKET
template: SELECT WIDTH_BUCKET(value, 0, 1000, 20) as bucket, MIN(value) as bucket_min, MAX(value) as bucket_max, COUNT(*) as frequency, COUNT(*) * 1.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER () as pct FROM metrics GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
anti_patterns: using equal-width bins (WIDTH_BUCKET) for highly skewed data — equal-frequency bins (NTILE) give more meaningful segments for long-tail distributions

title: A/B test conversion significance (two-proportion z-test)
template: WITH variants AS (SELECT variant, SUM(users) as n, SUM(conversions) as x, SUM(conversions) * 1.0 / NULLIF(SUM(users), 0) as p FROM ab_results GROUP BY 1), calc AS (SELECT MAX(CASE WHEN variant='control' THEN p END) as p_c, MAX(CASE WHEN variant='treatment' THEN p END) as p_t, MAX(CASE WHEN variant='control' THEN n END) as n_c, MAX(CASE WHEN variant='treatment' THEN n END) as n_t FROM variants) SELECT p_c, p_t, (p_t - p_c) / NULLIF(p_c, 0) as relative_lift, (p_t - p_c) / NULLIF(SQRT(((p_c*n_c + p_t*n_t)/(n_c+n_t)) * (1 - (p_c*n_c + p_t*n_t)/(n_c+n_t)) * (1.0/n_c + 1.0/n_t)), 0) as z_score, CASE WHEN ABS((p_t - p_c) / NULLIF(SQRT(((p_c*n_c + p_t*n_t)/(n_c+n_t)) * (1 - (p_c*n_c + p_t*n_t)/(n_c+n_t)) * (1.0/n_c + 1.0/n_t)), 0)) > 2.576 THEN '99%' WHEN ABS((p_t - p_c) / NULLIF(SQRT(((p_c*n_c + p_t*n_t)/(n_c+n_t)) * (1 - (p_c*n_c + p_t*n_t)/(n_c+n_t)) * (1.0/n_c + 1.0/n_t)), 0)) > 1.96 THEN '95%' ELSE 'not significant' END as significance FROM calc
anti_patterns: stopping the test as soon as p < 0.05 is reached (peeking) — inflate Type I error rate significantly; define sample size upfront using a power calculator

title: Median and mode calculation
template: SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY value) as median, (SELECT value FROM metrics GROUP BY value ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1) as mode, AVG(value) as mean FROM metrics
anti_patterns: using AVG for salary, revenue, or LTV reporting — mean is always distorted by top percentile; median is the right summary statistic

title: Rolling z-score for time-series anomaly detection
template: SELECT date, value, AVG(value) OVER (ORDER BY date ROWS BETWEEN 29 PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING) as rolling_mean, STDDEV(value) OVER (ORDER BY date ROWS BETWEEN 29 PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING) as rolling_stddev, (value - AVG(value) OVER (ORDER BY date ROWS BETWEEN 29 PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING)) / NULLIF(STDDEV(value) OVER (ORDER BY date ROWS BETWEEN 29 PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING), 0) as rolling_zscore FROM daily_metrics
anti_patterns: including the current row in the rolling mean — current point must not influence its own z-score; use ROWS BETWEEN N PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING
